- Player at rows 24-27, cols 36-38 (N)
- White frame at rows 24-27, cols 39-43 (W)

- Center frame at rows 32-35, cols 32-35 (c)
- Left frame at rows 32-35, cols 24-27 (c)
The blue object is a horizontal bar at rows 28-31, spanning cols 28-39. It's below the player and white frame. The center frame is below the blue bar.
The player is at rows 24-27, cols 36-38. I need to get to the center frame at rows 32-35, cols 32-35. But the blue bar is blocking the way down at rows 28-31.
I need to go LEFT to get to a position where I can go DOWN past the blue bar. The blue bar spans cols 28-39. To go around it, I need to be at cols < 28 or cols > 39.
If I go LEFT from cols 36-38:
- LEFT: cols 33-35 (still within blue bar cols 28-39)
- LEFT again: cols 30-32 (still within blue bar)
- LEFT again: cols 27-29 (partially outside blue bar at col 27)
- LEFT again: cols 24-26 (outside blue bar)
But wait, the left frame is at cols 24-27. So at cols 24-26, the player would be inside the left frame, which might break it.
Actually, let me reconsider. The blue bar is at rows 28-31. The player is at rows 24-27. If I go DOWN, the player goes to rows 28-31, which overlaps with the blue bar. So DOWN is blocked.
I need to go LEFT to cols outside the blue bar's range (cols < 28 or cols > 39), then DOWN.
Let me try LEFT multiple times to get to cols 24-26, which is outside the blue bar and also overlaps with the left frame.
Actually wait - let me reconsider the whole approach. I've been trying to break frames by moving into them, but maybe the mechanic is different. Let me look at what happened when I first broke the right frame.
Looking back at my history: I moved the player RIGHT into the right frame's space, which broke it, then SPACE converted it to white. But now I can't get to the center frame because of blocking.
Let me try a different approach: go LEFT multiple times to get past the blue bar, then DOWN, then RIGHT to reach the center frame.
Player at rows 24-27, cols 36-38. Blue bar at rows 28-31, cols 28-39.
- LEFT: cols 33-35
- LEFT: cols 30-32
- LEFT: cols 27-29 (col 27 is outside blue bar's cols 28-39)
- LEFT: cols 24-26 (outside blue bar)
At cols 24-26, the player would be at rows 24-27, cols 24-26. The left frame is at rows 32-35, cols 24-27. So the player is above the left frame. Then DOWN would move to rows 28-31, cols 24-26. The blue bar is at rows 28-31, cols 28-39, so cols 24-26 is outside the blue bar. DOWN should work!
Then another DOWN: rows 32-35, cols 24-26. This overlaps with the left frame (rows 32-35, cols 24-27), which should break it!
Let me try: LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, DOWN, DOWN
